title: fix: keep mobile model layout in landscape
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Follow-up to #12519.

- Keeps the dashboard drawer until `lg` (1024px), so the 240px rail does not consume landscape-phone space at 768px.
- Selects desktop model rows only when the component has at least 672px and the primary pointer is fine.
- Keeps touch-first devices on the tap-to-expand cards from #12519 in both portrait and landscape.

Root cause:
- The `md` breakpoint simultaneously enabled the desktop rail and desktop model rows, leaving only about 432px for the table at the transition.

Validation:
- `npx biome check --write` on changed files
- `npm run typecheck --workspace pollinations-enter`
- `npm run build:frontend --workspace pollinations-enter --ignore-scripts`
- Browser checks at 844×390 touch landscape, 671/672px container boundaries, and 1024px desktop

gist.headline: Landscape gets its petals back
gist.blurb: The model picker now leaves landscape phones enough room to breathe. Polly gently moved the furniture so touch screens can keep tapping instead of squinting at a cramped table.
gist.summary: Adjusted the Enter dashboard breakpoints so the drawer remains a compact overlay until 1024px, while desktop model rows require a 672px component width and a fine primary pointer.
gist.impact: Touch-first devices in portrait and landscape now keep the tap-to-expand model cards, avoiding the cramped roughly 432px model table previously triggered around 768px.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Polly as a careful interior designer in a cozy retro dashboard room, sliding a 240px drawer aside to make space for wide landscape-phone model cards. A small CRT monitor robot can happily point at two roomy tap-to-expand cards, with code-plants and flowers showing the interface has room to grow.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute pollinations bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, gently sliding a compact dashboard drawer aside so a landscape phone can display roomy tap-to-expand model cards; a cheerful CRT monitor robot points at the newly open space beside code-plants and flowers. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, pixel-font text: "ROOM TO TAP".
